data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description:
Hyperliquid launches voting mechanism for delisting on-chain assets, the first proposal will delist MYRO perpetual contract ARK Invest currently holds over $550 million worth of Coinbase shares The Ethereum ICO whale that has been silent for 7 years transferred 1,700 ETH to Binance
US Senator questions Trump's WLFI stablecoin regulatory risks
US SEC meets with Wintermute representatives to discuss solutions to crypto regulatory issues

data.title: Tesla's Musk-Led Government Efficiency Department Gains SEC Access
data.description: According to Odaily, the government efficiency department led by Tesla CEO Elon Musk has been granted access to the systems and data of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). The SEC plans to establish a liaison team to collaborate with the department, although the specific intentions remain unclear. Previously, the department attempted to shut down USAID and the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), leading to lawsuits alleging illegal actions. In January, U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order authorizing the department to reduce costs, but Musk's rapid approach has raised concerns among officials and lawmakers. SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce declined to comment, while new chairman candidate Paul Atkins expressed willingness to cooperate with the department.

data.title: xAI Acquires X in All-Stock Deal Valued at $80 Billion
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Elon Musk announced that xAI has acquired X through an all-stock transaction. The deal values xAI at $80 billion and X at $33 billion, accounting for $45 billion minus $12 billion in debt.

data.title: Dominari Holdings Buys $2 Million of BlackRock’s Bitcoin ETF
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Wealth management company Dominari Holdings announced that it will use part of its cash reserves to purchase $2 million of BlackRock iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) shares. The company is located in Trump Tower in New York, and its advisory board includes Trump's sons Eric Trump and Donald Trump Jr. Dominari does not hold Bitcoin directly, but obtains exposure through regulated ETFs to simplify compliance and accounting. This move reflects the Trump family's in-depth layout in the crypto field, and its son Donald Trump Jr. has recently actively participated in multiple crypto projects. (CoinDesk)

data.title: U.S. House of Representatives re-introduces encryption bill to combat illegal activities
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: U.S. Representatives Zach Nunn (Republican) and Jim Himes (Democratic) resubmitted the Fintech Protection Act on Thursday, which was passed by the House of Representatives last year but did not make progress in the Senate. The new bill proposes the establishment of an interdepartmental working group, including the Department of Justice, the Treasury Department, the FBI and crypto industry experts, to combat illegal financing and terrorist activities on digital asset platforms. This bipartisan bill is supported by the Trump administration and emphasizes that the United States needs to balance security and innovation to maintain its global financial leadership. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Arthur Hayes and BitMEX co-founder pardoned by Trump
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News White House officials confirmed that US President Trump pardoned Arthur Hayes, former CEO of the crypto exchange BitMEX, and his co-founders Samuel Reed and Benjamin Delo. The pardon was signed on Thursday. In 2020, the US Department of Justice accused BitMEX and four related personnel of violating the Bank Secrecy Act because they allowed anonymous transactions and failed to implement KYC requirements. Hayes and others pleaded guilty and were sentenced to probation and fines respectively. The exchange also pleaded guilty last year. Delo said that this pardon proved his innocence and was grateful to Trump. (Coindesk)

data.title: FDIC removes pre-screening requirement for banks’ crypto activities
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) issued a new policy to cancel the requirement set in 2022 that banks must obtain prior approval for crypto activities. Previously, the regulation caused banks to be disconnected from the digital asset industry due to delays in approval. Before the new guidance was issued, the head of the FDIC appointed by Trump promoted a crypto-friendly shift. FDIC Acting Chairman Travis Hill said that this move opened a new chapter in the wrong strategies of the past three years, and banks can now conduct crypto business after assessing the risks themselves. Similar adjustments are also seen in the recent revocation of similar restrictions by the OCC. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Coinbase Adds Keyboard Cat (KEYCAT) to Listing Roadmap
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to the official announcement of Coinbase, it has added Keyboard Cat (KEYCAT) to the coin listing roadmap.

data.title: Murad: A new era for Memecoin is coming
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Memecoin KOL Murad posted on the X platform, "A new era of Memecoin is coming. People are realizing that excessive gambling will only get you nothing. Now is the time to join Memecoin, which truly has a mission, community, and belief."

data.title: Is “Banana Zone” Still Relevant To The Price Of Bitcoin?
data.description:
Bitcoin’s widely discussed “banana zone” appears to be developing differently this cycle compared to previous bull markets, prompting questions about the next move for the price of Bitcoin.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/03/28/is-banana-zone-still-relevant-to-the-price-of-bitcoin/
